top_stocks.description:
• Celestica (CLS) is an electronics manufacturing services (EMS) provider and data-center infrastructure solutions company, serving customers across connectivity, cloud, and enterprise/communications end markets.
• Market cap/size classification: large-cap contract manufacturer/EMS (commonly categorized as a large-cap in U.S. markets).
• Key competitive advantage: scaled global manufacturing footprint plus design/manufacturing capabilities oriented to data-center and advanced technology programs (including hyperscaler-driven infrastructure).
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-01-28: Celestica reported Q4 and full-year 2025 results and raised its annual outlook, highlighting strong momentum into 2026.
• 2026-01-29: Celestica’s Q4 2025 earnings materials/coverage cited major strength in revenue (Q4 2025 revenue reported at $3.65B, +44% YoY) and improved outlook commentary for 2026.
• 2026-01-29 to 2026-01-31: Coverage around the earnings cycle emphasized a larger 2026 capital investment plan (capex) to support customer roadmaps for next-generation data-center infrastructure.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=42.11 and PEG=0.58 — the PEG below 1 suggests growth may be priced reasonably relative to earnings growth, though the absolute P/E remains elevated versus many industrials.
• Profitability: ROE=0.4049% and Profit Margin=0.0672% — margins/returns appear modest; the valuation implies the market is paying more for forward growth and operating leverage than for current profitability.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.4046% (as provided) is positive but looks low in magnitude; interpreting this alongside the investor metrics suggests expectations may rely on stronger actual business momentum than the single-digit/percent-decimal growth figure alone.
• Quality: Investor Score=90/100 — indicates strong investor sentiment/quality factors in your screener; combined with elevated ROIC (27.08%) it supports a “quality-with-growth-option” view even if current ROE and margin look thin.

top_stocks.description:
• SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) is a Chile-based mining/chemical producer best known for lithium and iodine, with the ADR traded in the U.S. as SQM.
• Market cap/size classification: large-cap commodities/chemicals producer (commonly categorized as large-cap in ADR screeners).
• Key competitive advantage: integrated position in lithium brine operations and established production/logistics footprint in Chile, giving scale and cost/volume leverage versus smaller producers.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-02 (coverage): SQM presented fourth-quarter 2025 results highlighting record lithium sales volumes (66.2 thousand metric tons in Q4 2025) alongside lithium price/volume dynamics.
• 2026-03-01 (coverage): Follow-up commentary discussed an earnings rebound theme tied to strong sales volumes and how the bull case may be changing.
• 2026-02 to 2026-03 (related coverage): Reports referenced lithium demand signals and contract/volume behavior impacting near-term guidance and pricing assumptions for 2026.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=38.44 and PEG=0.31 — the low PEG suggests earnings may be recovering faster than the P/E implies (or that forward growth is expected to be stronger than current earnings), though commodity cyclicality makes valuation less stable.
• Profitability: Profit Margin=0.1284% and ROE=0.1081% — current profitability/returns as provided look low, which is typical for cyclical commodity-linked earnings during normalization; however ROIC=5.90% is also relatively modest.
• Growth: EPS this year=1.8381% and EPS next 5Y=0.3872% (as provided) — implies some positive trajectory, but the very low/precision-style growth figures suggest you should treat this as a “cycle recovery” thesis rather than steady compounding.
• Quality: Investor Score=85/100 — indicates solid overall investor-quality assessment in your model; the core thesis likely depends on lithium supply/demand and price stabilization rather than purely operational margin expansion.

top_stocks.description:
• Lam Research (LRCX) is a leading semiconductor equipment supplier focused on wafer fabrication processes (notably etch, deposition, and related services), serving memory and logic manufacturers.
• Market cap/size classification: large-cap global semiconductor equipment company.
• Key competitive advantage: strong process technology portfolio and installed-base service/upgrade leverage, positioning Lam to benefit from leading-edge node and AI-related memory/compute buildouts.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-01-28: Lam Research reported financial results for the quarter ended 2025-12-28, with investor-relations materials describing performance and business update.
• 2026-01-28 (coverage): Earnings-call coverage noted confidence in continuing to outperform through portfolio strength and customer demand trends.
• 2026-03-12 (coverage): Recent commentary highlighted stock strength over the prior three months and cited expectations for strong 2026 growth drivers linked to advanced packaging and AI-driven memory demand.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=47.84 and PEG=1.32 — PEG above 1 suggests growth may not fully justify the P/E, so valuation looks demanding if semiconductor capex cycles soften.
• Profitability: Profit Margin=0.3022% and ROIC=44.78% — despite the relatively low profit-margin figure as provided, ROIC is very strong, indicating efficient capital deployment and high-quality business economics.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.2502% (as provided) — implies moderate growth; with PEG>1, the market may be pricing more confidence than the long-term EPS growth rate alone.
• Quality: Investor Score=85/100 — supports a durable, high-quality position; given ROIC strength, the key question is whether the current cycle sustains enough earnings growth to defend the premium multiple.

top_stocks.description:
• Jabil (JBL) is an electronics manufacturing services (EMS) and supply-chain solutions company that builds products for a range of end markets including data centers, networking/communications, and consumer/industrial devices.
• Market cap/size classification: large-cap global EMS/industrial technology services firm.
• Key competitive advantage: manufacturing scale plus end-to-end engineering/support capabilities, enabling customers to outsource complex product supply chains and accelerate launches.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-03-18: Jabil announced it would release its second quarter of fiscal year 2026 financials on Wednesday, March 18, 2026, before the market opened.
• 2026-03-18 (coverage): Reporting around the earnings cycle indicated Jabil raised full-year profit and revenue forecasts, linking demand to AI data center infrastructure.
• 2026-03-18 (company/IR materials): Earnings announcement set materials included the scheduled timing and standard investor-relations details for the quarter results presentation/release.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=37.99 and PEG=0.97 — PEG near 1 suggests valuation is roughly aligned with expected growth in your screener assumptions.
• Profitability: Profit Margin=0.0248% and ROE=0.5988% — margins as provided look thin (typical for EMS), while ROE is modest; this points to the importance of volume/operating leverage rather than high standalone margins.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.2022% (as provided) — indicates moderate forward growth; with PEG below/near 1, expectations appear achievable if end-market demand holds.
• Quality: Investor Score=85/100 — supports a relatively strong investor-quality profile; competitive positioning likely depends on maintaining customer mix (including data-center infrastructure) and cost execution through cycles.

top_stocks.description:
• Phibro Animal Health (PAHC) is an animal health and mineral nutrition company providing products for animal health, nutrition, and related solutions (feed additives and therapeutics/health products depending on segment).
• Market cap/size classification: mid-cap specialty healthcare/animal health company.
• Key competitive advantage: niche specialization in animal health and nutrition products plus product/portfolio coverage that can benefit from demand stability and trade/production relationships in animal agriculture supply chains.
top_stocks.latest_news:
• 2026-02-04 (company/IR): Phibro announced second-quarter results (quarter ended 2025-12-31) and updated financial guidance for the year ending 2026-06-30.
• 2026-02-05 to 2026-02-06 (coverage): Earnings commentary highlighted an animal-health-led performance and guidance raised theme tied to stronger results.
• 2026-02-05 (presentation/coverage): Reports referenced a Q2 2026 presentation and improvements that supported updated outlook messaging.
top_stocks.why_selected:
• Valuation: P/E=25.28 and PEG=0.81 — valuation looks more reasonable than many growth-sensitive healthcare/consumer-adjacent names, and PEG<1 suggests growth may justify the multiple.
• Profitability: Profit Margin=0.0629% and ROIC=8.56% — margins are not extremely high, but ROIC is moderate; this can fit a specialty business where growth and mix shift matter more than peak margin levels.
• Growth: EPS next 5Y=0.2104% (as provided) and EPS this year=0.4474% — suggests a positive growth trajectory consistent with a guidance-revision and improvement narrative.
• Quality: Investor Score=85/100 — indicates solid overall quality in your model; with reasonable valuation and recent guidance update, the key diligence item is whether guidance uplift is durable and not one-off.
